title: Phucagrostis rotundata
intro: There is no information available on the plant Phucagrostis rotundata.
taxonomy: Phucagrostis rotundata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Triticeae. The genus Phucagrostis is part of the subfamily Pooideae. This classification suggests a close relationship with other grasses within the family Poaceae. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Phucagrostis rotundata.
parts: Phucagrostis rotundata is not a recognized scientific name for any plant species. However, based on the given name, I found that it might be a misnomer or typo of  Phragmites australis. If Phragmites australis is the intended plant, then the medicinal parts of it typically include its rhizomes, leaves, and seeds. Rhizomes of Phragmites australis contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and seeds of this plant may also have medicinal properties, but further research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
